hero.title: 3 Days in Kochi.
hero.titleAccent: Colonial Charm, Spice Trails and Kathakali.
hero.intro: Fort Kochi is where Kerala's story is easiest to read. Portuguese, Dutch and British history layered over a working harbour, spice warehouses, art cafes, and the hypnotic theatre of Kathakali by night.
hero.heroImageAlt: Fort Kochi Chinese fishing nets at sunset

overview.heading: 3 Days Kerala Culture, Beauty & Kathakali: The Full Picture
body: Kochi, often called India's first European township, is one of the most atmospheric corners of South India. The old quarter of Fort Kochi is a walkable maze of colonial buildings, churches, antique shops and art galleries, fringed by the famous Chinese fishing nets that have lined the harbour for centuries.

This 3 day Kerala culture package from Malaysia is private and relaxed. You explore Fort Kochi and Mattancherry at your own pace, browse the spice markets, and end with a traditional Kathakali performance, the elaborate dance-drama that is one of Kerala's defining art forms.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Fort Kochi's Colonial Quarter
items.body: Wander streets layered with Dutch, Portuguese and British history. St Francis Church, the Dutch Palace, the iconic Chinese fishing nets at sunset, and cafes housed in old spice warehouses.

items.title: Mattancherry & the Spice Markets
items.body: The old spice trade quarter, including Jew Town and the Paradesi Synagogue, is full of antique shops, spice merchants and the scents that once drew Europeans across the seas.

items.title: A Kathakali Performance
items.body: Kathakali is Kerala's spectacular classical dance-drama, with extraordinary makeup, costumes and expression. Arrive early to watch the performers transform, then experience the show itself.

days.title: Arrive Kochi
days.description: Met at Cochin International Airport and transferred to your Fort Kochi hotel. Evening free to catch the Chinese fishing nets at sunset.
suggestedActivities: Sunset at the Chinese fishing nets

days.title: Fort Kochi, Mattancherry & Kathakali
days.description: A full day exploring the old town's heritage and spice quarter, ending with an evening Kathakali show.
suggestedActivities: St Francis Church and Santa Cruz Basilica

days.title: Free Morning, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. A final wander or some spice and craft shopping before your airport transfer.

whyBook.heading: Kerala, the Way Locals Know It
whyBook.body: Fort Kochi is best explored slowly, and a local guide unlocks the stories behind the spice trade, the synagogue and the fishing nets. Dhesu has arranged Kerala holidays for Malaysians since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing

sections.heading: Kerala Culture Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
This short Kochi break is perfect for travellers who want a taste of Kerala's heritage and arts without committing to a long backwaters trip. Fort Kochi packs centuries of history into a small, walkable area, and the Kathakali performance is a genuine cultural highlight.

The private vehicle and guide mean you move at your own rhythm, from morning church visits to sunset at the fishing nets.

Want the full Kerala experience with backwaters and tea hills? See our 4 day, 5 day and 6 day Kerala packages.

sections.heading: What Makes Fort Kochi Special
sections.body:
Few places in India wear their history so openly. The spice trade that drew the Portuguese, Dutch and British is written into the buildings, the synagogue, the churches and the warehouses now turned into cafes and galleries.

It is also one of India's most creative corners, home to the Kochi-Muziris Biennale, with a relaxed, artistic energy you do not find in the bigger cities.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Cochin International Airport (COK). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Kochi?
items.answer: September to March is ideal, with pleasant weather and lower humidity. The monsoon (June to August) is lush and atmospheric but wet. Kochi is warm and humid year-round given its coastal position.

items.question: Can I add backwaters or Munnar to this trip?
items.answer: Yes. Alleppey's backwaters are a couple of hours away and Munnar's tea hills a scenic drive inland. Our 4 day, 5 day and 6 day Kerala packages combine Kochi with backwaters, Munnar and more.

items.question: Is Kerala food vegetarian friendly?
items.answer: Very. Kerala cuisine offers abundant vegetarian options alongside its famous seafood. The traditional sadya, a feast served on a banana leaf, is entirely vegetarian and a must-try.
